description_long: This is a pair of metal pliers with a utilitarian, industrial appearance, featuring two elongated handles and a robust, angular jaw. The handles are straight and appear to be made from a single piece of metal, lacking any visible ergonomic padding or insulation. The jaws are asymmetrical, with one side forming a hooked, curved gripping surface and the other a flat, straight edge, suitable for gripping or turning objects. There is a visible pivot joint near the head, allowing the jaws to open and close, and a cutout in the metal near the pivot for weight reduction or design. The surface of the pliers shows signs of wear and discoloration, suggesting frequent use or exposure to the elements. The overall finish is matte and slightly rough, with a utilitarian, no-frills design typical of tools found in urban garages or construction sites.
description: A rugged, straight-handled metal pliers with a hooked jaw and visible pivot joint, showing signs of wear and a matte, utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, the hooked jaw, and the cutout near the pivot.
description_view_1: Edge-on view revealing the thin profile of the pliers and the alignment of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the straight handle and the angular, hooked jaw design.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again showing the thinness and the straight alignment of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, uninsulated metal handles with no ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Angular, hooked jaw with one curved gripping surface and one flat edge; lacks serrations or wire cutter.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed pivot joint allowing standard opening and closing motion; no adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
